PLAN_STATE: Perform variable elimination for P(B9=1 | A7=1, B1=1, B5=1, C7=1, C2=1). Evidence fixes B1=1, B5=1, C2=1, A7=1, C7=1. Query is B9. Eliminable variables: A1, A2, A3, B2, B3, B4, C1, C3, D0, BR01, BR12, A4, A5, A6, B6, B7, B8, C4, C5, C6. Strategy: work through the network layer by layer, using evidence to reduce CPTs, then eliminate variables in an order that keeps factor sizes small. Key dependencies for B9: D0, BR12, A6. BR12 depends on B4, C2(evidence). A6 depends on A4, A5. D0 depends on A3, B4, C3. Will trace through all factors carefully, summing out eliminable variables one at a time. Order: A1, C1, B2, B3, B4, BR01, A2, A3, C3, D0, BR12, A4, A5, A6, C4, C5, C6, B6, B7, B8. Compute numerator (B9=1) and denominator (sum over B9) to get conditional probability.
